posts.contentSnippet:
Initially, the A330 didn't sell that well because its range was only just over 5,500 nautical miles. That's useful for transatlantic routes between the US East Coast to central Europe but not much else in terms of long-distance flights. Hence the push for the smaller A330-200 with its range of 6,400 nautical miles, which DID become a very successful model for Airbus because its range meant it could fly many transoceanic routes.

You said "If I was to guess…". That's incorrect. ALL —— HYPOTHETICALS —— IN ENGLISH use the verb WERE:

posts.contentSnippet: Malaysia Airlines has always have the best hospitality, friendly crew, hardworking, passionate about their job. But sadly they are limited to what they can afford to give you the best service imaginable, simply put, not that much capital unlike their competitors like Singapore, Qatar and so on. Just imagine if they have the capital like Qatar Airways did + the hardworking and very nice crew members that they already excel at, it would be an airline that is unbeatable. I've flown with those airli

posts.contentSnippet: I’m glad SW have assigned seats. It wasn’t fair I would pay for the early seating just to find out people that have C and D get on first. It was about 20 people in wheelchairs and as soon as the agents said if you can walk go ahead aboard if not you have to wait. Everyone stood up except one. Then I would see people in wheelchairs and be walking and dancing on a cruise. | https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yoY0OIv_aOE | @nicoleb3103
